lede: A look at January 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Bill Maher and André-Marie Ampère, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Iceland
events.desc: A protest movement in Iceland culminates as the 2009 Icelandic financial crisis protests start.

events.title: Sudan
events.desc: Sudan's government imposes Islamic law nationwide, worsening the civil war between the country's Muslim north and Christian south.

events.title: Ronald Reagan
events.desc: Twenty minutes after Ronald Reagan is inaugurated as the 40th President of the United States of America, Iran releases 52 American hostages.

events.title: Nazi Germany
events.desc: World War II: Germany begins the evacuation of 1.8 million people from East Prussia, a task which will take nearly two months.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: At the Wannsee Conference held in the Berlin suburb of Wannsee, senior Nazi German officials discuss the implementation of the "Final Solution to the Jewish question".

events.title: Bucharest
events.desc: A German officer is killed in Bucharest, Romania, sparking a rebellion and pogrom by the Iron Guard, killing 125 Jews and 30 soldiers.

events.title: Hong Kong Island
events.desc: Hong Kong Island is occupied by the British during the First Opium War.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of his cantata Meine Seufzer, meine Tränen (My sighs, my tears), BWV 13, for the second Sunday after Epiphany.

events.title: Edward Balliol
events.desc: Edward Balliol surrenders his claim to the Scottish throne to Edward III in exchange for an English pension.

events.title: Lalli
events.desc: Finnish peasant Lalli kills English clergyman Henry, the Bishop of Turku, on the ice of Lake Köyliö.

births.name: Bill Maher
births.line: American comedian, political commentator, media critic, television host, and producer

births.name: Nikki Haley
births.line: American accountant and politician, 116th Governor of South Carolina

deaths.text: Audrey Hepburn, British actress and humanitarian activist
Bettina von Arnim, German author, illustrator, and composer
Emperor Shizong of Jin, Chinese emperor of Jin
